name: Pot Roast with Mustard Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven, brown the roast at medium-high heat, roasting it in oil for about 2 inches of coverage on all sides. Pour off any rendered fat.
2. Add water, beef broth, white wine, minced garlic, thyme, and black pepper to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil.
3. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18 to 22 minutes per pound, or until a meat thermometer registers 140°F when inserted into the roast.
4. Remove the roast from the pot and place it on a serving platter. Reserve the liquid in the pot.
5. Cover the roast tightly with oil and let it stand for 10 minutes before carving. (During this time, the internal temperature will rise to about 150°F for medium-rare.)
6. Measure out 1 cup of the poaching liquid from the roast pan. In a small saucepan, dissolve cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of this liquid.
7. Add the remaining 3 cups of poaching liquid, along with Dijon mustard and chopped parsley to the saucepan. Cook over medium heat until the sauce is clear and slightly thickened, about 3 to 5 minutes.
8. Remove the roast from the oil and carve into thin slices. Serve the roast with the mustard sauce.
Note: The remaining poaching liquid can be used to make a rich onion soup.
